title: FY 2026 Federal Appropriations for the City of Reno
resolved_text:
Memorandum
DATE: February 11, 2026
TO: Honorable Mayor and Council
THROUGH: Jackie Bryant, City Manager
FROM: Nic Ciccone, Government Affairs Manager
  Calli Wilsey, Policy and Strategy Director
DEPT: Office of Policy & Strategy
SUBJECT: FY 2026 Federal Appropriations for the City of Reno (Community Project Funding Requests Update)

President Donald Trump signed a spending package on Tuesday, February 3 to fund federal government operations and programs through September 2026. Congress passed the package of appropriations bills earlier on Tuesday. The approved bills include direct funding to government and nonprofit entities for specific projects through the Community Project Funding (CPF) process, formerly referred to as earmarks.

With the support and advocacy of Senators Cortez Masto and Rosen as well as Congressman Amodei, the City of Reno is set to receive several million dollars for various projects outlined below.

Project Award
Reno Police Department – Real Time Information Center $1,200,000
Evelyn Mount Northeast Community Center – Wellness Remodel $3,000,000
Total $4,200,000

Additionally, several infrastructure projects in Reno will benefit from awards to the City of Reno's regional partners, RTC Washoe and the City of Sparks.
• RTC Washoe partnered with the City of Reno, the City of Sparks, and Washoe County to develop a joint $6,000,000 proposal to fund local street rehabilitation projects – $2,000,000 will go towards the Marsh Avenue and Sharon Way neighborhood street rehabilitation project.

• The City of Sparks received an award of $3,773,000 for phase one design and construction services to acquire backup power systems for the Truckee Meadows Water Reclamation Facility, a shared facility between Reno and Sparks.

Summary of City of Reno Awards
Reno Police Department – Real Time Information Center
The City of Reno Police Department's Real Time Information Center (RTIC) project will allow the Reno Police Department to purchase equipment and software to further enhance the Real Time Information Center's ability to embrace intelligence-led policing, provide enhanced information for response or deterrence of calls for service, increased investigatory capability, and overall greater public safety. The proposal to allocate funding for the establishment of a Real Time Information Center heralds a significant technological advancement with far-reaching implications for law enforcement operations and public safety. By centralizing data sources and providing real-time situational awareness, the Real Time Information Center stands to revolutionize the Reno Police Department's ability to respond effectively to incidents and protect the community the Reno Police Department serves.
Strategic Plan Goal: Public Safety
CPF Requested Amount: $1,200,000
CPF Award Amount: $1,200,000

Evelyn Mount Northeast Community Center – Wellness Remodel
The proposed project includes the modification and remodeling of the aging Evelyn Mount Northeast Community Center to establish a comprehensive whole health and wellness hub catering to the diverse needs of youth, families, and seniors. Located in an area lacking access to these services, the Wellness Remodel request seeks to modify the Evelyn Mount Northeast Community Center into a vibrant space offering a wide array of amenities and services aimed at fostering physical, mental, and social well-being. The Wellness Remodel will modify the north wing of the Evelyn Mount Northeast Community Center to allow increased capacity for wellness programming. The Wellness Remodel project will remove or modify walls, flooring, HVAC ducts, plumbing, and electrical.
Strategic Plan Goal: Economic & Community Development; Arts, Parks, and Historical Resources
CPF Requested Amount: $3,150,000
CPF Award Amount: $3,000,000

fields.authors: Nic Ciccone, Government Affairs Manager; Calli Wilsey, Policy and Strategy Director
fields.tldr: President Trump signed the FY 2026 federal spending package on February 3, 2026, which includes Community Project Funding awards totaling $4,200,000 directly to the City of Reno for two projects. The Reno Police Department's Real Time Information Center will receive $1,200,000 for equipment and software to enhance intelligence-led policing, and the Evelyn Mount Northeast Community Center will receive $3,000,000 for a wellness remodel. Additional regional awards include $2,000,000 toward Reno neighborhood street rehabilitation via an RTC Washoe joint proposal, and $3,773,000 to the City of Sparks for backup power systems at the shared Truckee Meadows Water Reclamation Facility.
fields.key_stats: Direct City of Reno CPF awards: $4,200,000 total ($1,200,000 for RPD Real Time Information Center; $3,000,000 for Evelyn Mount Northeast Community Center Wellness Remodel). Regional partner awards: $6,000,000 joint RTC Washoe street rehabilitation proposal (with $2,000,000 allocated to Marsh Avenue and Sharon Way); $3,773,000 to City of Sparks for Truckee Meadows Water Reclamation Facility backup power. Evelyn Mount CPF requested amount was $3,150,000 vs. $3,000,000 awarded.
fields.key_decisions: No council action items explicitly requested; memo is an informational update on confirmed federal appropriations awards secured with support from Senators Cortez Masto and Rosen and Congressman Amodei.
fields.action_items: No specific action items listed; staff to proceed with implementation of awarded projects per CPF requirements.

projects: Reno Police Department – Real Time Information Center (RTIC)
Evelyn Mount Northeast Community Center – Wellness Remodel
Marsh Avenue and Sharon Way Neighborhood Street Rehabilitation
Truckee Meadows Water Reclamation Facility Backup Power Systems
dollar_amounts: $1,200,000 – RPD Real Time Information Center CPF award
$3,000,000 – Evelyn Mount Northeast Community Center Wellness Remodel CPF award
$3,150,000 – Evelyn Mount Wellness Remodel CPF requested amount
$4,200,000 – Total direct City of Reno CPF awards
$6,000,000 – RTC Washoe joint street rehabilitation proposal
$2,000,000 – City of Reno share for Marsh Avenue and Sharon Way street rehabilitation
$3,773,000 – City of Sparks award for Truckee Meadows Water Reclamation Facility backup power
dates: February 3, 2026 – President Trump signed the federal spending package
September 2026 – End of federal government funding period covered by the spending package
people: Calli Wilsey, Policy and Strategy Director
